subTitle: Straight answers. If yours isn't here, just ask.

faqs.question: How does pricing work?
faqs.answer: Every project gets a fixed quote before we start. No hourly billing, no surprises halfway through. The price depends on what you actually need, which is why we always start with a conversation. If your budget's tight, say so — there's usually a smart way to stage things.

faqs.question: What do you need from me to get started?
faqs.answer: Less than you'd think. A rough idea of what you want, and any content you already have — text, photos, your logo. If you don't have those yet, that's fine. Sorting them out is part of the process, and I'll tell you exactly what's needed and when.

faqs.question: Can you fix my existing site, or do you only build new ones?
faqs.answer: Both. Sometimes a site just needs a tidy-up and a speed boost, not a rebuild. I'll take a look and tell you honestly which one you need — even if the answer is the cheap option.

faqs.question: What happens after the site goes live?
faqs.answer: I don't vanish. Every project includes post-launch support for any issues that come up, and I'll show you how to make simple updates yourself. If you'd rather not touch it at all, that's what the monthly retainer is for.

faqs.question: I'm not technical. Is that a problem?
faqs.answer: Not even slightly — most of my clients aren't. You explain your business; I handle the web side and explain it back in plain English. Hosting, domains, email — I can set all of it up, and everything stays in your name.
